subtopics.title: Two Sum
subtopics.description: Find pair that sums to target

data.content:
# Two Sum

Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers that add up to `target`.

## Example
```
Input:  nums = [2, 7, 11, 15], target = 9
Output: [0, 1]
```

## Constraints
- 2 ≤ nums.length ≤ 10⁴
- -10⁹ ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10⁹
- Exactly one valid solution

## Approaches
### Brute Force (O(n²))
Check every pair.

### Hash Map (O(n))
Store seen values; for each element check if complement exists.

subtopics.title: Sliding Window Maximum
subtopics.description: Maximum sum subarray of fixed size K

data.content:
# Sliding Window

Find maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size `k`.

## Example
```
Input:  nums = [2, 1, 5, 1, 3, 2], k = 3
Output: 9
```

Window slides right by one each step, adding new element and removing leftmost.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n)
- Space: O(1)

# Binary Search

Find target value in sorted array in O(log n).

Divide array in half each iteration, discard the half that cannot contain target.

## Key Points
- Array must be sorted
- Three pointers: left, mid, right
- Terminates when left > right

## Complexity
- Time: O(log n)
- Space: O(1)

subtopics.title: Valid Palindrome
subtopics.description: Check if string reads same forward and backward

data.content:
# Valid Palindrome

A phrase is a palindrome if it reads the same forward and backward after converting to lowercase and removing non-alphanumeric characters.

## Example
```
Input:  "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
Output: true
```

## Approach
Two pointers from both ends, skip non-alphanumeric, compare lowercase.

tests.input: ["A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"]

subtopics.title: Valid Anagram
subtopics.description: Check if two strings are anagrams

data.content:
# Valid Anagram

Given two strings `s` and `t`, return true if `t` is an anagram of `s`.

## Example
```
Input:  s = "anagram", t = "nagaram"
Output: true
```

## Approach
Count character frequencies. Both strings must have same count for each character.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n)
- Space: O(k) where k = alphabet size

subtopics.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
subtopics.description: Find longest contiguous substring with all unique characters

data.content:
# Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters

Given a string `s`, find length of longest substring without repeating characters.

## Example
```
Input:  "abcabcbb"
Output: 3 ("abc")
```

## Approach
Sliding window with hash set. Expand right pointer, if duplicate found, shrink left until duplicate removed.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n)
- Space: O(min(n, k)) where k = charset size

subtopics.title: Bubble Sort
subtopics.description: Simple comparison sort — bubble largest to end

data.content:
# Bubble Sort

Repeatedly step through array, compare adjacent elements, swap if wrong order.

Each pass bubbles the largest unsorted element to its correct position.

## Example
```
Initial: [5, 1, 4, 2, 8]
Pass 1:  [1, 4, 2, 5, 8]
Pass 2:  [1, 2, 4, 5, 8]
```

## Complexity
- Time: O(n²) worst, O(n) best (optimized)
- Space: O(1)

subtopics.title: Merge Sort
subtopics.description: Divide and conquer — split, sort, merge

data.content:
# Merge Sort

Divide array into halves, recursively sort each half, merge sorted halves.

## Key Insight
A single-element array is always sorted. Build up from there.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n log n)
- Space: O(n) auxiliary

subtopics.title: Quick Sort
subtopics.description: Divide and conquer with pivot partitioning

data.content:
# Quick Sort

Pick a pivot, partition array around pivot, recursively sort partitions.

## Partitioning (Lomuto)
Elements < pivot go left, > pivot go right. Pivot ends in correct position.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n log n) average, O(n²) worst (bad pivot)
- Space: O(log n) stack

subtopics.title: Contains Duplicate
subtopics.description: Check if array has any duplicate values

data.content:
# Contains Duplicate

Given integer array `nums`, return true if any value appears at least twice.

## Example
```
Input:  [1, 2, 3, 1]
Output: true
```

## Approach
Use a Set. For each element, if already in set → duplicate found.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n)
- Space: O(n)

subtopics.title: Maximum Subarray (Kadane)
subtopics.description: Find contiguous subarray with largest sum

data.content:
# Maximum Subarray (Kadane's Algorithm)

Find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum.

## Example
```
Input:  [-2, 1, -3, 4, -1, 2, 1, -5, 4]
Output: 6 (subarray [4, -1, 2, 1])
```

## Kadane's Insight
At each position, decide: extend current subarray or start fresh?
`currentSum = max(nums[i], currentSum + nums[i])`

## Complexity
- Time: O(n)
- Space: O(1)

subtopics.title: Climbing Stairs
subtopics.description: Count ways to reach top climbing 1 or 2 steps

data.content:
# Climbing Stairs

You climb a staircase with `n` steps. Each step you can go up 1 or 2 steps. How many distinct ways to reach top?

## Example
```
Input:  n = 3
Output: 3
```
Ways: (1,1,1), (1,2), (2,1)

## Recurrence
`dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]`
Same as Fibonacci.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n)
- Space: O(1)
